onboarding.heading: Learn English
onboarding.tagline: Master vocabulary every day. No login. Just learn.
onboarding.question: How many words would you like to learn in this session?
onboarding.footer: Progress is saved automatically. Words you miss are prioritized in the next session.

aiLoadingFacts: Waiting for AI to respond...
English borrows words from more than 350 other languages.
The word "set" has hundreds of meanings in major English dictionaries.
A pangram is a sentence that uses all 26 letters of the alphabet.
"Goodbye" comes from the older phrase "God be with ye."
English spelling is hard because pronunciation changed faster than writing.
Phrasal verbs can change meaning completely with one short preposition.
"I am" is one of the shortest complete sentences in English.
The most common letter in written English is "e."
English uses stress to distinguish some nouns and verbs.
Major English dictionaries add new words every year.
The word "bookkeeper" has three pairs of double letters in a row.
English has no official language academy that controls all correct usage.
The sentence "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog" uses every letter.
Many English words changed meaning over centuries, not just spelling.
"Awful" once meant worthy of awe, not necessarily bad.
Old English sounded much closer to German than modern English does.
English adjectives usually follow a hidden order: opinion, size, age, shape, color.
The word "nice" once meant foolish or ignorant before it became positive.
Some English nouns become verbs without changing form, like "email" or "text."
Silent letters often remain because spelling preserves older pronunciations.

settings.heading: Settings
settings.resetConfirm: Are you sure? This cannot be undone.
